name: Seasons
description: Cycle-based model that uses recurring monthly, weekly, or session tendencies as directional bias and timing context.

context: Start with a seasonal hypothesis, then verify current structure supports it.
Use the cycle as bias, not as a blind entry trigger.
Mark the nearby levels where the cycle could accelerate or fail.
setup: Historical tendency favors the same direction in the current period.
Price builds a continuation pattern in line with that tendency.
No major opposing event has invalidated the usual flow.
trigger: Enter on break of local continuation structure in the direction of the cycle.
Use pullback confirmation if the cycle is early and volatility is mixed.
Skip direct entry if the move already traveled most of its usual distance.
confirmation: Current session behavior matches the expected seasonal window.
Related market context is not sharply opposing the thesis.
Trend structure stays orderly with higher lows or lower highs.
invalidation: Current period behaves opposite the usual cycle with clean acceptance.
Catalyst-driven repricing overrides the historical pattern.
Range compression kills follow-through before entry matures.
noTrade: Sample size for the seasonal idea is weak or anecdotal.
The move is already extended into major higher-timeframe structure.
Upcoming data can fully reset the cycle narrative.

stopLoss: Place the stop outside the local structure that invalidates the cycle bias.
Use the opposite side of the pullback or continuation base.
Do not widen the stop just because the cycle should work.
takeProfit: Target the usual seasonal range extension first.
Hold the remainder only while structure confirms the cycle is unfolding.
Take profit ahead of known reversal windows if the pattern usually fades there.
timeExit: Exit when the seasonal window closes.
Flatten before the next major macro event if it changes the regime.
Do not hold beyond the period that gives the edge.
earlyExit: Current cycle diverges sharply from expected flow.
Structure breaks against the thesis before target progression begins.
Correlated market context flips hard against the idea.
